name: Modern monolithic silhouette
description: A strictly geometric dwelling read as one or two interlocking rectangular volumes with a flat (or barely sloped) roof, capping the building as a clean horizontal plane. Walls are taut planar surfaces broken by large glazed openings — full-height windows, ribbon glazing, or sliding-door bays — that emphasize mass over ornament. The footprint is a rectangle, or two boxes of slightly different heights joined along one edge to break the silhouette without compromising orthogonality. Upper floors may cantilever 1-2 blocks beyond the ground floor to cast a shadow line and signal the structural frame. Common in Bauhaus and International-Style references, contemporary villas, and minimalist gallery-houses; the entrance sits on a long side, offset from center to preserve the asymmetric composition.
placement_rules: Footprint is a single rectangle or two rectangles joined along one full edge — never an L, T, or freeform shape.
Aspect ratio (length:width) stays between 1.0 and 1.8; squarer reads as a cube pavilion, longer as a slab block.
Roof is strictly flat or sloped at most 1 block of rise across the whole span — no gables, hips, or pitched forms.
Walls are unbroken planar surfaces; window openings are large (2-4 blocks wide, 2-3 tall) and aligned in horizontal bands or full-height bays.
Main entrance is placed on a long side, offset from the geometric center by at least 2 blocks to preserve asymmetric composition.
When two volumes are used, the upper or rear volume may cantilever 1-2 blocks beyond the lower box on one face; supports are implied by the box itself, no visible columns.
Building height ranges from 1 storey (4-5 blocks) for pavilions to 3 storeys (9-10 blocks) for stacked villas before reading as a tower.

modern.notes: Crisp white volumes with light-gray accent box; flat slab roof line; full-height glass bays on the long side facing the view.

minimalist.notes: Monochrome quartz envelope; near-invisible trim; openings reduced to a few oversized rectangles to maximize blank wall.

fantasy.notes: Off-world Bauhaus pavilion; pale stone volumes set against landscape; same orthogonal logic but with mineral palette.
